skill.name: amazon-reviews-api-skill
skill.description: Extract Amazon product review data by ASIN through the BrowserAct Amazon Reviews API workflow.
skill.summary: Runs a BrowserAct workflow to collect Amazon reviews for a supplied ASIN.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The static critical heuristic is not confirmed as malicious after review. Network access and environment secret usage are expected for the BrowserAct API workflow, but this community skill still carries medium risk because it sends an API key to a third-party service and includes guidance that may lead users to paste credentials into chat. No prompt injection or obfuscated payload was found.

medium_findings.title: Authenticated Third-Party API Requests
medium_findings.description: Verdict: true positive. The Python helper reads a BrowserAct API key and sends it as a Bearer token to BrowserAct workflow endpoints. This is expected for the skill, but it exposes a user secret to an external service and should require clear user consent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly obtains BROWSERACT_API_KEY and uses it in Authorization headers for requests to api.browseract.com. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the issue is exposure risk rather than confirmed malicious exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: API Key Disclosure Guidance
medium_findings.description: Verdict: true positive. The skill tells the user to provide the API key if the environment variable is missing. Asking users to paste credentials into an agent chat can leak secrets into logs or transcripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly references BROWSERACT_API_KEY setup and the script prints guidance to provide the key or set an environment variable. The risk depends on how the agent handles the secret, but the unsafe disclosure path is present.

medium_findings.title: Result Retrieval Block Is Unreachable
medium_findings.description: The script breaks out of the polling loop after a finished status, then immediately prints a timeout error and returns before the result retrieval block. This is primarily a reliability issue, but it can cause users to retry and consume API quota without receiving results.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The control flow is explicit: after the loop exits, line 67 returns None before the result retrieval code at lines 70-83 can run. This makes the advertised successful output path unreachable.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive for direct code execution. The static analyzer matched backticks and a bash example in documentation, but SKILL.md does not execute commands itself. The agent is instructed to run a local Python helper, which is normal for this skill but should be done with a trusted ASIN argument.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is markdown documentation and code formatting, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. There is still an operational risk because the documented workflow asks the agent to run the bundled script.

low_findings.title: Static Critical Combination Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: Verdict: false positive for confirmed malicious intent. The dangerous combination finding is explained by a documented local script, expected BrowserAct network calls, and API key authentication. No evidence found of prompt injection, obfuscation, credential theft to an unrelated endpoint, or hidden destructive behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found the network and credential access align with the advertised BrowserAct API workflow. The only executable code is the Python helper, and no malicious endpoint or obfuscated payload was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Authenticated Third-Party API Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: Verdict: true positive. The Python helper reads a BrowserAct API key and sends it as a Bearer token to BrowserAct workflow endpoints. This is expected for the skill, but it exposes a user secret to an external service and should require clear user consent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code clearly obtains BROWSERACT_API_KEY and uses it in Authorization headers for requests to api.browseract.com. The behavior matches the skill purpose, so the issue is exposure risk rather than confirmed malicious exfiltration.

dangerous_patterns.title: API Key Disclosure Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: Verdict: true positive. The skill tells the user to provide the API key if the environment variable is missing. Asking users to paste credentials into an agent chat can leak secrets into logs or transcripts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly references BROWSERACT_API_KEY setup and the script prints guidance to provide the key or set an environment variable. The risk depends on how the agent handles the secret, but the unsafe disclosure path is present.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Verdict: false positive for direct code execution. The static analyzer matched backticks and a bash example in documentation, but SKILL.md does not execute commands itself. The agent is instructed to run a local Python helper, which is normal for this skill but should be done with a trusted ASIN argument.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The matched content is markdown documentation and code formatting, not Ruby or shell backtick execution. There is still an operational risk because the documented workflow asks the agent to run the bundled script.

content.value_statement: Amazon review collection is slow when teams must build crawlers or copy data manually. This skill runs a BrowserAct workflow that collects structured review data from a product ASIN.
actual_capabilities: Accepts an Amazon ASIN as the main input value.
Starts a BrowserAct workflow template for Amazon review extraction.
Reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment for API authentication.
Polls BrowserAct task status and prints timestamped progress messages.
Reports missing keys, invalid authorization, failed tasks, and polling errors.
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key before the script can run.
Sends the ASIN and API key authorization header to BrowserAct endpoints.
The current script control flow can prevent successful result retrieval.
Review availability depends on Amazon pages and the BrowserAct workflow.

use_cases.title: Compare Competing Products
use_cases.description: Collect review text and ratings for competitor ASINs to identify strengths, defects, and buyer complaints.

use_cases.title: Monitor Product Feedback
use_cases.description: Gather recent customer comments for a product and summarize themes that need product or support action.

use_cases.title: Prepare Review Research Data
use_cases.description: Create a review dataset for sentiment, verified purchase, variant, or country-level analysis.

prompt_templates.title: Get Reviews for One ASIN
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Amazon reviews skill for ASIN B07TS6R1SF. Show the available review fields and summarize the main customer themes.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Customer Complaints
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract reviews for ASIN B08N5WRWJ6. Focus on low ratings, repeated complaints, and issues reported by verified buyers.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Variants
prompt_templates.prompt: Collect Amazon reviews for this ASIN and compare feedback by product variant, rating, country, and verified purchase status.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Research Summary
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the review extraction workflow for this ASIN, then produce an executive summary with sentiment themes, rating distribution, notable quotes, and data limitations.

output_examples.output: A list of review entries with reviewer name, rating, title, review text, publication date, country, variant, and verified purchase status.

output_examples.input: Complaint analysis for a competitor product

output_examples.output: A concise report covering positive themes, negative themes, rating signals, verified buyer patterns, and limitations of the extracted data.

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable instead of pasting it into chat.
Verify that each ASIN belongs to the intended Amazon product before running extraction.
Review BrowserAct and Amazon terms before using collected data commercially.
anti_patterns: Do not paste private API keys into shared conversations or saved prompts.
Do not treat extracted reviews as complete market truth without sampling checks.
Do not retry failed workflow runs many times without checking the error cause.

faq.question: What input does this skill need?
faq.answer: It needs an Amazon ASIN and a valid BrowserAct API key in BROWSERACT_API_KEY.

faq.question: Does it require an Amazon account?
faq.answer: The skill documentation says no Amazon login is required for the BrowserAct workflow.

faq.question: What review fields can it return?
faq.answer: The documentation lists reviewer name, profile link, rating, title, text, date, country, variant, and verified purchase status.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses a third-party API and handles an API key.

faq.question: Why can a run fail?
faq.answer: Runs can fail because of a missing key, invalid authorization, BrowserAct task failure, network errors, or polling timeout.

faq.question: Can it analyze sentiment by itself?
faq.answer: It collects review data. Sentiment analysis must be performed by the agent after extraction.
